avalancheActivity.highlights:
The snowpack will be prone to triggering above approximately 1800 m. Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow represent the main danger. Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate defensive route selection.
As a consequence of warming during the day, the likelihood of moist avalanches being released will increase on south facing slopes below approximately 2300 m.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The fresh snow of yesterday and the wind slabs must be evaluated with care and prudence in all aspects. Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released even easily by individual winter sport participants, especially on steep west, north and east facing slopes at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions. Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach large size. The avalanche prone locations are prevalent barely recognisable for beginners.
Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack serve as an alarm indicating the danger.
As a consequence of warming during the day, the likelihood of moist and wet avalanches being released will increase on south facing slopes below approximately 2300 m. Avalanches can occur in particular in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Towards its base, the snowpack is faceted and weak. Avalanches can be released in deeper layers also.
In addition the fresh wind slabs adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls are easily triggered.
Towards its surface, the snowpack is hard and its surface has a crust that is strong in many cases.

tendency.highlights: As a consequence of warming during the day the prevalence of the avalanche prone locations will increase as the day progresses.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weakly bonded old snow represents the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can be released in the old snowpack and reach large size. This applies even in case of a single winter sport participant. The avalanche prone locations are to be found on west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2000 m. As a consequence of warming during the day and the solar radiation, the likelihood of slab avalanches being released will increase a little also on steep sunny slopes at elevated altitudes. Caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack. Whumpfing sounds can indicate the danger. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible in isolated cases.

As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation only isolated mostly small wet avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes. This applies especially above approximately 2000 m.

Towards its surface, the snowpack consists of faceted crystals, especially on shady slopes.

Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be quite good. The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust. The weather conditions as the day progresses will give rise to slight softening of the snowpack in particular on very steep sunny slopes.

tendency.highlights:
Weakly bonded old snow requires caution. Single winter sport participants can release avalanches as before.

Slight increase in danger of wet avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

avalancheActivity.comment:
The wind slabs can be released easily in some places in particular on steep shady slopes. Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution and restraint.
As a consequence of warming wet loose snow avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The snowpack is not distinctly layered and its surface has a melt-freeze crust that is strong in many cases. The surface of the snowpack will soften during the day.
The spring-like weather conditions will give rise to unfavourable bonding of the snowpack in some cases on very steep sunny slopes. These conditions will cause a weakening of the snowpack in gullies and bowls and behind abrupt changes in terrain. As a consequence of highly fluctuating temperatures and solar radiation a treacherous avalanche situation will develop. This applies in particular on steep south, southwest and west facing slopes above the tree line.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet and gliding avalanches are the main danger.

snowpackStructure.comment: In gullies and bowls above approximately 1800 m a lot of snow is lying. The spring-like weather conditions gave rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack. These conditions will cause a gradual weakening of the snowpack also at intermediate and high altitudes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Slight increase in danger of moist and wet avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day. Individual gliding avalanches can also occur.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The surface of the snowpack is frozen, but not to a significant depth and will soften earlier than the day before. The weather conditions will cause a slight rise in the danger of moist avalanches as the day progresses in particular at intermediate altitudes.

In particular above approximately 2200 m: Weak layers in the old snowpack can still be released in some place by winter sport participants, after an overcast night in particular.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In particular in the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

Intermediate and high altitudes: Weak layers exist in the snowpack especially on west, north and northeast facing slopes.

In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind. In places that are protected from the wind in all altitude zones a lot of snow is lying for the time of year.

avalancheActivity.highlights:
The wind slabs can be released easily in some places in particular on steep shady slopes. Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution and restraint.
As a consequence of warming moist small and medium sized avalanches are to be expected.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Dry avalanches can be released in deeper layers also.
As a consequence of warming during the day, the likelihood of moist and wet avalanches being released will increase in particular on steep north and east facing slopes below approximately 2500 m.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Precarious weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
The upper section of the snowpack is hard and its surface has a strong crust.

tendency.highlights: As a consequence of warming during the day the prevalence of the avalanche prone locations will increase as the day progresses.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weakly bonded old snow requires caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can in some cases be released in the old snowpack and reach quite a large size on west, north and east facing slopes. This applies even in case of a single winter sport participant in some cases. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular above approximately 2000 m. Caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.

Individual mostly small wet avalanches are possible below approximately 2600 m.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Shady slopes: Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes. This applies especially above approximately 2000 m.

Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be good. The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust. The weather conditions as the day progresses will give rise to softening of the snowpack in particular on very steep sunny slopes.

tendency.highlights: Weakly bonded old snow requires caution. Slight increase in danger of wet avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

avalancheActivity.comment: The dangerous spots are found on steep slopes, especially grassy ones, as well as in areas near the ridges and at the base of rock walls, also due to the possibility of debris falling from above. As the day progresses the likelihood of moist avalanches being released will increase gradually in particular on sunny slopes. Gliding avalanches can also occur. The wind slabs are in isolated cases prone to triggering. Backcountry touring calls for meticulous route selection.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be quite good over a wide area. The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust. The weather conditions as the day progresses will give rise to softening of the snowpack, in particular at low and intermediate altitudes.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be mostly sunny.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Individual weak layers exist in the centre of the old snowpack above approximately 2500 m.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released especially by large additional loads especially above approximately 2500 m. This applies in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, and on very steep slopes at intermediate and high altitudes. In the event of an overcast night caution is to be exercised.
In particular at low and intermediate altitudes and in steep rocky terrain individual mostly small moist and wet avalanches are possible as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

Early morning: There is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust and will soften earlier than the day before.

Especially steep west, north and northeast facing slopes high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Weak layers exist in the old snowpack.

In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weakly bonded old snow requires caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can in some cases be released in the old snowpack and reach large size in isolated cases on west, north and east facing slopes. This applies even in case of a single winter sport participant in some cases. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular above approximately 2000 m. Caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example. As a consequence of warming during the day and the solar radiation, the likelihood of slab avalanches being released will increase a little also on steep sunny slopes at elevated altitudes.

Only isolated mostly small wet loose snow avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes. This applies especially above approximately 2000 m.

Towards its surface, the snowpack consists of faceted crystals, especially on shady slopes.

Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be quite good. The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust. The weather conditions as the day progresses will give rise to slight softening of the snowpack in particular on very steep sunny slopes.

tendency.highlights: Weakly bonded old snow requires caution. Slight increase in danger of wet avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Individual weak layers exist in the centre of the old snowpack above approximately 2500 m.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released especially by large additional loads especially above approximately 2500 m. Caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, and on very steep slopes at intermediate and high altitudes.
Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to a loss of strength within the snowpack in some cases in particular on sunny slopes at low and intermediate altitudes.
In the morning, There is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Especially steep sunny slopes low and intermediate altitudes: The old snowpack is hard and its surface has a strong melt-freeze crust.
Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise as the day progresses to softening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes at low and intermediate altitudes.
Especially steep west, north and northeast facing slopes high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack.

In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

region.name: B - VALLI DI GRESSONEY, AYAS, CHAMPORCHER

avalancheActivity.highlights: The backcountry touring conditions are generally favourable.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The surface of the snowpack will freeze to form a strong crust only at high altitudes, in the event of an overcast night in particular.
Slight increase in danger as a consequence of warming during the day. In particular at intermediate altitudes individual mostly small moist and wet avalanches are possible as the day progresses, in particular in case of releases originating from extremely steep starting zones that still retain some snow.
Weak layers exist deeper in the snowpack. Avalanches can in very isolated cases be released in deep layers and reach medium size in isolated cases. These avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular in areas where the snow cover is rather shallow and in little used backcountry terrain.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The weather conditions fostered a strengthening of the snowpack in particular on sunny slopes. Towards its surface, the snowpack is largely stable and its surface has a melt-freeze crust that is strong in many cases. In steep terrain there is a danger of falling here.
Especially steep shady slopes high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Weak layers exist in the old snowpack. Towards its surface, the snowpack consists of faceted crystals; its surface consists of faceted crystals.
Adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas as well as at high altitude snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

tendency.highlights: The backcountry and freeriding conditions are quite favourable. In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface. As a consequence of warming during the day there will be no slight decrease in the danger of moist and wet avalanches in the late morning.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack can still be released in isolated cases.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can in very isolated cases be released in the old snowpack and reach medium size in isolated cases on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes. This applies in case of a single winter sport participant.

As a consequence of warming during the day individual wet avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Shady slopes: Avalanche prone weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

Sunny slopes: The weather conditions gave rise to moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2500 m.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be mild. Slight increase in danger of wet avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Slight increase in danger of moist and wet avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released in isolated cases and mostly by large additional loads at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions. This applies at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, and on extremely steep slopes at intermediate and high altitudes.
As a consequence of warming and solar radiation, the natural avalanche activity will slowly increase.
Very early morning: There is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The snowpack will be quite stable.

In particular low and intermediate altitudes: The surface of the snowpack is frozen, but not to a significant depth and will soften earlier than the day before.
In the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

avalancheActivity.comment: Above approximately 1500 m medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large moist and wet avalanches are possible.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack can still be released in isolated cases.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can in very isolated cases be released in the old snowpack and reach medium size in isolated cases on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes. This applies in case of a single winter sport participant.

As a consequence of warming during the day individual wet avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Shady slopes: Avalanche prone weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

Sunny slopes: The weather conditions gave rise to moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2500 m.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be mild. Slight increase in danger of wet avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation.
